rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (U.S.A.), N.A. VS. MARIANA TIFFER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Defendant'S Claim Of Exemption Per Ex Parte Order Of May 8, 2013 Vacating Ruling Of May 3, 2013 That Granted Defendant'S Claim Of Exemption In Part And (2) Recalendaring The Claim Of Exemption Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 13, 2013, Line 1. Hearing On Defendant's Claim Of Exemption. The claim is denied. First, defendant filled out the wrong form (wage garnishment). Second, defendant does not cite a statutory exemption. If she is relying on CCP 704.070(b)(2) [paid wages exempt], defendant fails to show that the money the sheriff is holding is paid wages or otherwise exempt. Third, defendant miscalculates her take home pay. She receives $2724.23/month (excluding worker's compensation payments) and that sum exceeds her monthly expenses ($2,482). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: WYDE VOICE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC.,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Compliance With Certain Topics In Pltf'S Subpoena For Personal Appearance Of Google, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Thursday, June 13, 2013, Line1: Plaintiff WYDE VOICE LLC'S Motion to Compel Compliance with Certain Topics in Plaintiff's Subpoena for Personal Appearance of Google, Inc. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The Court has reviewed all of the papers and is of the view: (1) additional meet-and-confer discussions would be beneficial, and (2) to the extent the parties are unwilling or unable productively to engage in further meet-and-confer discussions, the appointment of a discovery referee is appropriate. The parties should be prepared to discuss, in addition to the merits of the motion, their views with respect to additional meet-and-confer discussions and the appointment, by stipulation or order, of a discovery referee. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WYDE VOICE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC.,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Seal Documents In Support Of Gips' Motion To Compel Fcc To Provide Pmk And Produce Documents
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Thursday, June 13, 2013, Line 1: Defendant GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS, INC.'S Motion to Seal Documents in Support of GIPS' Motion to Compel FCC to Provide PMK and Produce Documents Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The unopposed motion is brought pursuant to California Rule of Court 2.551, which does not apply to discovery motions. California Rule of Court 2.550(a)(3). The Court construes the motion as a request to seal records the parties have designated as confidential and which appear to contain confidential and/or private information. The motion is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHNA PECOT et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Joinder And Joinder To Def San Francisco Deputy Sheriffs' Association'S Mtn To Disqualify Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 13, 2013, Line 2. PLAINTIFFS JOHNA PECOT, THOMAS ARATA, RICH OWYANG, STEPHEN TILTON, JOSEPH LEAKE, OSCAR TAYLOR'S Joinder To Defendant San Francisco Deputy Sheriffs' Association's Motion To Disqualify Counsel. Denied. SFDSA has not met is burden to disqualify Mr. Murray as counsel. See State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. v. Federal Ins. Co. (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1431. In all four actions ("Pecot I," "Pecot II," the Federal Lanham Act action, and the "conversion" action), SFDSA was represented by separate counsel other than Mr. Murray. Mr. Murray's communications and invoices are consistent with his representation of the individual defendants and his belief under the disputed fee agreement that SFDSA paid for his services. Further, SFDSA has not demonstrated a substantial relationship between the subjects of the current representation of the individual defendants and Mr. Murray's alleged former representation of a sheriff in an administrative appeal and in a corporate matter. Further, SFDSA has not demonstrated that Mr. Murray had access to confidential information such that it is a basis to disqualify him as counsel in this case. SFDSA has not shown that Mr. Bosworth's representation of the Foundation is a basis to disqualify Mr. Murray in this action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SHELLEY WIENKE, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF THE VS. CHASE HOME FINANCE, LLC., A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 13, 2013, Line 3. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained in part. As to the first cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff was granted leave to amend her Second Amended Complaint to allege Defendant Chase unequivocally failed to contact Plaintiff prior to recording the Notice of Default and Plaintiff did not do so. As to the second cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff was granted leave to amend to allege what portion of the contract was breached and Plaintiff failed to do so. As to the third, fourth and fifth. As to the seventh cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. This cause of action was foreclosed b y Goems v. Countrywide Home Loans, Inc. (2011) 192 Cal. App. 4th 1149, 1152 n. 7, cert. denied 132 S.Ct. 419 (2011). As to the eighth and tenth causes of action, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to plead ultimate facts supporting the causes of action. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CAROLYN BENGTSON VS. SAN FRANCISCO SUPPLY MASTER, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Submission To Written Pscych Testing
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 13, 2013, Line 8. DEFENDANT CORD INVESTMENT COMPAY'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Submission To Written Pscych Testing. Granted. Defendants have shown good cause to justify the administration of the Millon Clinical Multiaxial Inventory-III and Minnesota Multiphasic Personality Inventory-2 psychiatric examinations. (Vinson v. Superior Court (1987) 43 Cal.3d 833, 843 - 844. See Emmett Decl., Exhibit H (Declaration of Dr. Mark Strassberg, 6, 7.) Plaintiff is ordered to attend an independent psychiatric examination with Dr. Mark Streassberg, M.D., on a mutually agreed upon date. The examination shall include these two written tests. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: WYDE VOICE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al VS. GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Production Of Documents By Deft Google, Inc. In Response To Pltf'S First Req. For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Thursday, June 13, 2013, Line 3: Plaintiffs WYDE VOICE LLC'S and FREE CONFRENCING CORPORATION'S Motion to Compel Production of Documents by Defendant Google, Inc. in Response to Plaintiff's First Request for Production of Documents Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The Court has reviewed all of the papers and is of the view: (1) additional meet-and-confer discussions would be beneficial, and (2) to the extent the parties are unwilling or unable productively to engage in further meet-and-confer discussions, the appointment of a discovery referee is appropriate. The parties should be prepared to discuss, in addition to the merits of the motion, their views with respect to additional meet-and-confer discussions and the appointment, by stipulation or order, of a discovery referee. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH J. RADER JR VS. SUN LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY OF CANADA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 13, 2013, Line 17. DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty, which the complaint does not now do. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN BRUCE VS. YOUNG K. CHO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 13, 2013, Line 18. CROSS DEFENDANT STEVEN BRUCE'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant Steven Bruce's demurrer to the Complaint is overruled in its entirety. Mr. Bruce's request for judicial notice is granted. Even if a small claims judgment could have res judicata or collateral estoppel effect, the record is not sufficiently clear to determine whether the issues litigated in small claims court are the issues being litigated in this case before the Superior Court. See Pitzen v. Superior Court (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1374, 1381). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
